LATTICE (2) product order reflexive + antisymmetric
LATTICE (3) meet=glb, join=lub, commutative + absorption (64² pairs)
LATTICE (4) A_id / A_fresh product-incomparability (M1.4 — no ⊥): id-up vs fresh-up incomparable
LATTICE (5) projectTier is monotone (order-preserving)
LATTICE (6) projectTier agrees with the realized §14 tier (identity+time)
LATTICE (6b) integrity floor unmet ⇒ NONE (INVALID upstream)
LATTICE (6c) LIVE verify tier == projectTier(emitted assurance) across LIGHT/HIGH/TOP (single source, no transcription gap)
LATTICE (7a) capAssurance downgrade-only (cap ≤ proven) + idempotent
LATTICE (7b) no ceiling ⇒ unchanged
LATTICE (7c) proven-TOP capped by no-trust-roots/no-domains ⇒ tier drops to LIGHT
LATTICE (8) missing/out-of-range axis ⇒ reject sentinel (fail-closed, total-by-return) + projectTier ⇒ NONE
LATTICE product = 64 states (2×4×4×2) — strength only, support is not a coordinate (M1.1)

PhA quorum: two sources in one domain → count 1

R3 IDENTITY: verify emits id(x̂) bound to the admitted snapshot it verified — the transcript is addressed by the returned id, not by a re-read of the raw input

R3 NESTED: a two-face NESTED genesis in a resolver opts/config graph → the output is a projection over the VERIFIED face, never the unsigned re-read (round-31 P0-01/02/03; admitOpts is shallow, nested untrusted docs are deep-admitted once)
R3 NON-BYPASS: a stateful Proxy answering the descriptor one value and [[Get]] another → INVALID, not a false VALID — verify reads the SAME face canon/identity reads

R3 ONE-CONTROL: admitOpts deep-admits EVERY nested opts/config DATA value once, so a resolver NOT individually patched (verifyEpochTransition) reads its nested doc ≤1 and cannot be shown a second face — the whole nested-doc re-read class is closed at ONE boundary (round-31)
R3 RESOLVER: resolveKeys admits its genesis ONCE — a two-face Proxy (signed A to verify, tampered B to the reducer) emits keys for the VERIFIED face or errors, NEVER the re-read face
R32 Horn≡projectTier: every identity×time cell — max Horn Tier == projectTier
R32 Horn≡projectTier: pinned identity derives NO TierHIGH (key-pinned, not name-bound)
R32 order calendar-valid still holds: real same-clock instants compare chronologically
R32 order cross-clock: two intervals on different clocks prove nothing → unproven
R32 order cross-kind: transparency-log wearing pow facts {substrate,position} → unproven (its coord is log_id/index)
R32 order inverted-interval: rfc3161-tsa not_before > not_after in one receipt → unproven
R32 order non-calendar: rfc3161-tsa not_before 9999-99-99T99:99:99Z (shape-valid, calendar-invalid) → unproven
R32 order transparency-log legit {log_id,index} orders within one log
R33 P0-01 EXTRA claim field → INVALID (exact claim keys)
R33 P0-01 EXTRA envelope field → INVALID (envelope exactly { claim, issuer_id, sig })
R33 P0-01 a well-typed transparency-log receipt is STILL VALID (kernel-aligned, no over-reject)
R33 P0-01 shape-valid IMPOSSIBLE issued_at 9999-99-99T99:99:99Z → INVALID (real calendar, not just regex)
R33 P0-01 signed transparency-log receipt with an EXTRA facts field → INVALID (closed per-kind facts, kernel-aligned)
R33 P0-01 unregistered proof_kind → INVALID (closed kind registry, own-key)
R33 P0-01 wrong per-kind facts (pow {substrate,position} worn by a transparency-log) → INVALID
R33 P0-02 issuer_id two-face Proxy → INVALID; issuer_id read from the ADMITTED snapshot R, never a raw re-read
R34 P0-01 a genesis authority carrying a non-canonical Pub32 alias roots NO authority (strict Pub32 before keyId)
R34 P0-01 a non-canonical Pub32 alias maps to the SAME key (keyId) but is not canonical
R34 P0-02 a checkpoint witness with an unsigned EXTRA sig field → INVALID (closed { body, sig } — no checkpoint-id malleability)
R34 P0-02 the genuine checkpoint is STILL VALID (kernel-aligned, no over-reject)
R34 P0-03 a uniqueness claim with an EXTRA signed field (observed_map_root ∉ the closed VOTE_CLAIM) is DROPPED → quorum not met
R35 P0-01 the genuine uniqueness quorum is STILL attested (no over-reject)
R35 P0-01 uniqueness sig.key_id ≠ issuer (admitSigner binds issuer===key_id===keyId(pub)) → NOT attested
R35 P0-02 recovery sig.key_id ≠ issuer (admitSigner binds issuer===key_id===keyId(pub)) → NOT recovered
R35 P0-03 no-fork GENUINE witness → ok (now in the closed-ADT sweep)
R35 P0-03 no-fork extra ENVELOPE field → ok:false (closed { claim, issuer_id, sig })
R35 P0-03 no-fork foreign sig.key_id → ok:false
R35 P0-03 no-fork open sig (alg:RSA + foreign key_id + extra wrapper field) → ok:false
R35 admitSigner gate: every sig-wrapper tampering on a genuine attestation → NOT attested; only the genuine wrapper passes
R35 no-fork self-declared valid_as_of in the signed claim → ok:false (assurance-never-self-declared; time is not a signer field)
R36 P1-01 admitAuthorityKey rejects a contradictory { key_id of A, pub of B } pair (via transition destination) — usable only if key_id === keyId(pub)
R36 P1-02 edVerifyStrict REJECTS a non-canonical Pub32 alias (same 32 bytes, different wire)
R36 P1-02 edVerifyStrict REJECTS a non-canonical Sig64 alias (same 64 bytes, different wire) — canonical enforced at the crypto leaf
R36 P1-02 edVerifyStrict verifies a genuine canonical Ed25519 signature
R37 P0-01 a GENUINE key-log terminality proof is still terminal (no over-reject)
R37 P0-01 key-log Merkle sibling "SHA256:ABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABABAB" (not a canonical hash) → not terminal, NO host throw
R37 P0-01 key-log Merkle sibling "not-a-hash" (not a canonical hash) → not terminal, NO host throw
R37 P0-01 key-log Merkle sibling "sha256:abababababababababababababababababababab" (not a canonical hash) → not terminal, NO host throw
R37 P0-01 key-log Merkle sibling 5 (not a canonical hash) → not terminal, NO host throw
R37 P0-01 key-log Merkle sibling [] (not a canonical hash) → not terminal, NO host throw
R37 P0-01 key-log Merkle sibling null (not a canonical hash) → not terminal, NO host throw
R37 P0-01 key-log Merkle sibling {} (not a canonical hash) → not terminal, NO host throw
R37 P1-01 joinAssurance of two VALID states still lifts (no over-reject)
R37/R39 assuranceLE with an out-of-domain operand → FALSE (an order we cannot establish is never asserted), never a throw
R37/R39 joinAssurance with an OUT-OF-DOMAIN operand → RETURNS the valid operand (⊥ contributes nothing, never synthesizes strength from garbage), never a throw
R37/R39 meetAssurance with an out-of-domain operand → RETURNS ⊥ (fail-closed, under-reports), never a throw
R38 P1-01 (R1/R3) assuranceState on a two-face Proxy emits the ADMITTED (first) face, not a stronger re-read
R38 P1-02 (R1) compareEvidenceOrder on a hostile Proxy operand → unproven, never a host throw
R38 P1-02 (R1) quorumTrustDomains on a Proxy list with a hostile Symbol.iterator → structured, never a host throw
R38 P1-03 (R4) verifyJson maxInputBytes below the ceiling still applies (a valid tighten is honored)
R38 P1-03 (R4) verifyJson maxInputBytes:Infinity → structured E-MALFORMED, never an expanded ceiling
R38/R39 (R1) assuranceState on a hostile getter → RETURNS a reject sentinel (a symbol, mirrors admitDeep→ADMIT_REJECT), never a throw
R39 P1-01 (R4) maxSupportedBytes:0 → E-MALFORMED, never a falsy-bypass of the capability check
R39 P1-01 (R4) maxSupportedBytes:Infinity → E-MALFORMED, never a disabled capability check
R39 P1-01 (R4) maxSupportedBytes:NaN → E-MALFORMED, never a falsy-bypass
R39 P1-01 (R4) refBudget:0 → E-MALFORMED (0 is not a positive integer of nodes)
R39 P1-01 (R4) refBudget:Infinity → E-MALFORMED (a refused budget FAILS CLOSED, never coalesces to the 256-node default)
R39 P1-02 (R1) axisRank on a non-axis key → -1 (total), never a `undefined.indexOf` throw
R39 P1-02 (R1) capAssurance with a HOSTILE consumer ceiling → RETURNS ⊥ (fail-closed cap), never a host throw
R39 P1-02 (R1) checkBounds on a HOSTILE doc → RETURNS a bounds refusal string, never a host throw

R4 CLOCK-OWNED: a hostile __nowMs in public opts is DEAD (never read) — it cannot expand the witness budget or flip resource_limit into a served-list HIGH (round-29 P0-02; ρ_v belongs to the verifier)

R4 MONOTONIC: the witness budget clock is a monotonic ELAPSED source (performance.now), non-decreasing across reads — a wall-clock/NTP rollback cannot rewind the deadline and disable the whole-op budget (round-30 P1-01; not a wall clock with a wrapper)
R40 P1-01 (R1) sync verify admits opts ONCE — verifyCore never re-reads the live opts Proxy (a two-face maxSupportedBytes trap fires 0×)
R40 P1-01 (R1) the two-face opts verdict is DETERMINISTIC (no compute-limit-then-skip; same input → same verdict)
R40 P1-02 (R1) every MALFORMED capAssurance ceiling (falsy scalar / non-record / array: false,0,"",NaN,[],1,"x",true) → ⊥ (projectTier NONE), never a preserved TOP
R40 P1-02 only a genuinely ABSENT ceiling (undefined/null) is identity; a partial record caps per axis
R41 P1-01 (R1) the admitted opts snapshot handed to a capability as `this` is FROZEN (inert) — a resolveRef cannot mutate policy (acceptConsumerOverride) to flip LIGHT→HIGH
R41 P1-02 (R1) resolveAuthority with a PRESENT falsy/scalar/array genesis → E-GENESIS (falsy ≠ absent), never a silent self-asserted
R41 P1-02 only a genuinely ABSENT genesis (undefined/null) is self-asserted; a valid genesis resolves (no over-reject)
R41 P1-02 pinnedKeys sibling — a present NON-ARRAY pin set → E-MALFORMED (a dropped restriction would silently accept any key); an array pins; absent is self-asserted
R41 P1-02 verify with a present malformed genesis → E-GENESIS (the verifyCore opts.genesis door, not only resolveAuthority)
R41 P1-02 verifyStream sibling (self-audit sweep) — a PRESENT falsy/scalar genesis or checkpoint in config → complete:none with a MALFORMED detail, never silently provisional
R42 P0-01 (R1/R3) resolveKeys DEEP-admits the key-log — a two-face entry (signed key B / unsigned key C) never authorizes C (structured error OR authorizes only B)
R42 P1-01 (R1) an unminted servedNoFork + acceptConsumerOverride does NOT reach HIGH (override_liftable:false), yet still ≠ corroborated (rc35-P0a)
R42 P1-01 the LEGIT explicit-axiom override still lifts (noForkConfirmed:true + acceptConsumerOverride:true → VALID:HIGH)
R42 P1-02 (R1) a wrong-typed GRANT boolean → E-MALFORMED (acceptConsumerOverride:"false" is truthy; noForkConfirmed:"yes")
R42 P1-02 resolveKeys rejects a PRESENT non-array keylog → E-MALFORMED (verifyStream delegates here, no longer coalescing false→[])
R43 P1-01 deriveCheckpointFreshness allowExperimentalAttested:"false" (a truthy non-boolean) → INVALID, never ENABLES the experimental attested rung
R43 P1-01 verifyStream requirePerFrameValid:0 (a falsy non-boolean) → structured malformed, never DISABLES per-frame X2 signature verification
R43 P1-02 resolveCadence keylog:false (present malformed selector) → E-MALFORMED, never coalesced to an empty (retirement-erasing) log
R43 P1-02 verifyAuthorityCheckpointChain genesis:false + a fallback genesisAuthority → NOT a silent VALID consumer-pin (a present malformed selector is rejected, never skipped to the fallback root)
R43 legit boolean policy values still honored (requirePerFrameValid:false, allowExperimentalAttested defaults) — no over-reject
R43 the RESTRICTION booleans are measured too — a coerced requireAuthoritative:0 → E-MALFORMED, never a silently DROPPED requirement (a real boolean still restricts)

R43/R44 STRUCTURAL: every require*/allow*/acceptConsumerOverride security-policy boolean in index.mjs + reference-checker.mjs is REGISTERED (a new grant flag fails until admitted + adversarially probed)
R43/R44 STRUCTURAL: no authority SELECTOR uses a coalesce (`Array.isArray(X)?X:d`, `X || {}`, `X || []`) — a present malformed selector is admitted, never silently emptied/defaulted

R44 P0-01 verifyAuthorityBundle injects NO threshold DEFAULT — the adapter passes the consumer policy through (no `Number.isInteger(...) ? ... : 2` fallback that manufactures a quorum the sole-checker refuses)
R44 P1-01 verifyAuthorityCheckpointChain rejects a PRESENT malformed authority selector (genesisAuthority:false / recoveryKeys:0) → E-MALFORMED, never silently overridden or coalesced to {}
RECOVERY 1-of-3 (below threshold) → chain INVALID(E-AUTHORITY)
RECOVERY 2-of-3 (lost key K1) authorizes replacement KR → chain VALID
RECOVERY conflicting replacements (non-identical claims) → no quorum (must agree on ONE)
RECOVERY does NOT bypass checkpoint validation (recovered signer, but malformed rotation → E-MALFORMED)
RECOVERY effective_sequence ≠ last+1 → not recovered (only the next checkpoint)
RECOVERY same signer twice → counts once → quorum not met
RECOVERY signer NOT in the genesis recovery set → not counted
RECOVERY stale last_accepted_checkpoint → not recovered (bound to the prior)

RECOVERY threshold-complete malformed replacement (BOTH signers agree on key_id ≠ keyId(pub)) → NOT recovered (admitAuthorityKey binds the pair; round-36 P1-01/P2-01 — the vacuous single-malformed vector could not see it)
RECOVERY valid 2-of-3 → replacement_authority + threshold + 2 signers
